title: 10 best herbal creams for stye

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with stye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredient, calendula, works to reduce redness and swelling associated with styes, allowing the eye to heal faster and more effectively. Additionally, calendula's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection causing the stye, preventing further irritation and promoting a smooth recovery. As a result, pot marigold creams provide a natural and effective solution for soothing and treating styes.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Pot marigold contains californianum, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Staphylococcus aureus, helping to prevent infection and promote healing of styes.

Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes, making them less noticeable and more comfortable to manage.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidants help protect the skin from free radical damage, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, allowing the cream to promote a healthy and soothing environment for the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against bacterial infections and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and oils that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers weighing approximately 200g for this recipe.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days.
Grind dried calendula flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine ground calendula powder with 300ml of coconut oil and 30g of beeswax in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until melted and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with stye because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that help reduce redness and swelling associated with styes. Additionally, aloe's natural antibacterial agents combat bacterial infections that can cause styes to form. By applying an herbal aloe cream directly to the affected area, it helps calm irritated skin, promotes healing, and reduces the risk of scarring, ultimately alleviating the discomfort and appearance of a stye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and alocemide, help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, promoting faster healing.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of aloe vera, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, help combat infections that may be contributing to the development or worsening of styes.

Wound-healing: Aloe vera's wound-healing properties, which include increasing collagen synthesis and improving blood flow, aid in the natural healing process of styes by promoting tissue repair and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from aloe leaves is excellent for soothing and calming irritated eyes and skin, making it a perfect base for creams to treat styes.
Rhyzomes: Aloe rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, promoting faster healing.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 ounces of pure aloe vera gel and mix it with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a clean glass bowl.
Add 5 drops each of tea tree oil and lavender oil to the mixture for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Combine 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a separate glass bowl then melt them in a double boiler.
Gradually add the melted wax mixture to the aloe vera gel mixture while stirring constantly until well combined and thickened.
Pour the herbal cream into an air-tight container and store it in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with stye because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb Echinacea, also known as purple coneflower, contains compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with styes. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial agents help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to stye formation. By soothing and calming the affected area, Kansas coneflower creams provide fast and effective relief from the discomfort and redness caused by styes, allowing for a faster recovery and reduced risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: The active compound echinacea, found in Kansas coneflower, has antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause styes by reducing the growth and proliferation of harmful bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamic acid, help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Immune-boosting properties: The antioxidants and other bioactive compounds present in Kansas coneflower cream may help stimulate the immune system to respond more effectively to infections, reducing the severity and duration of stye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of Kansas coneflower have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness associated with sty eyes.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds of Kansas coneflower are used to create soothing topical creams that calm the skin around the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine the echinacea powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar and stir well for 10 minutes.
Add 50 milliliters of beeswax pellets to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler or a warm water bath at 70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Remove the jar from the heat source and let the mixture cool and solidify at room temperature for 1 hour before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator at 4 degrees celsius for up to 6 months or until used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as tea tree oil and chamomile, work together to reduce swelling and redness associated with styes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting a healthy environment for the eye to heal naturally. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, germander creams provide effective relief from stye discomfort and promote faster healing.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: The cream's antibacterial properties help to combat the underlying bacterial infection that causes a sty, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Germander cream reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes, allowing the affected area to heal faster and more comfortably.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of Germander cream help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and slow down the healing process, promoting a healthier and more rapid recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against stye's redness and swelling.
Buds: Containing bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections causing stye.
Fruits: With their natural antibacterial properties, fruits of the germander plant can aid in preventing infection and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh teucrium chamaedrys leaves and clean them thoroughly by rinsing under cool running water.
Dry the cleaned teucrium chamaedrys leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for one hour or air-dry them for three days.
Combine one part of dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves with two parts of petroleum jelly and mix well until a uniform paste forms.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds to 1 minute, stirring every 15 seconds until smooth.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sunflower creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness associated with this common eye infection. The creams also contain antiseptic and antibacterial agents that combat bacterial overgrowth, promoting a healthy environment for the affected area to heal. Additionally, sunflower creams' soothing and moisturizing effects help calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting faster recovery. As a natural remedy, sunflower creams provide a gentle yet effective way to alleviate stye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sunflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes by reducing inflammation.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial agents in herbal sunflower creams, such as terpenes and fatty acids, work to combat the bacterial infection that causes styes, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal sunflower creams' antioxidant content helps protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of styes and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with styes.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and vitamins E and B, which help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helianthus annuus flowers and dry them in shade for at least 3 days to preserve potency.
Mix 20g of dried helianthus annuus flowers with 10ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar and store it for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, squeezing gently to extract oils and resinous compounds.
Combine 100ml of distilled water with 20g of glycerin and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the glycerin dissolves completely.
Slowly mix the helianthus annuus oil infusion with the glycerin-water solution, stirring constantly until a smooth cream forms after about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with the stye. When applied topically, chamomile cream calms the affected area, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications like scarring or eye irritation. Its gentle, non-irritating texture also makes it an effective treatment for sensitive eyes, providing long-lasting relief from the discomfort caused by this common ocular issue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, promoting healing and comfort.
Antimicrobial property: Chamomile's antimicrobial activity helps to combat the bacteria that cause styes, preventing infection and promoting resolution of the condition.
Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects help to reduce discomfort, itching, and irritation associated with styes, promoting a sense of relaxation and ease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Contain a unique blend of oils and compounds that provide moisturizing and protective benefits to the skin around the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them in the shade for 2 weeks.
Combine 50g of dried chamomile flowers with 100ml of cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the dark at room temperature for 4 hours to infuse the oil with the chamomile essence.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15-20 minutes until melted and well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream can reduce swelling and redness, making it easier for the body to fight off bacterial infections that cause sty. Additionally, the herb has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat bacteria that can exacerbate the condition. By applying St John's Wort cream topically, it can provide quick relief from discomfort and promote faster healing of the affected area.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes.
Hypericin: As an antimicrobial agent, hypericin can inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to the development of styes, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in St. John's Wort have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin around the affected area, reducing discomfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with styes.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort contain flavonoids, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating skin infections like styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material using a low-temperature oven at 60°f for 24 hours to preserve its medicinal properties.
Blend one part dried st john'swort herb with three parts coconut oil and mix well to create the base cream.
Add 10 drops of pure lavender essential oil per tablespoon of base cream for soothing and anti-inflammatory benefits.
Mix the blend thoroughly and pour into sterile jars, letting it mature at room temperature for 7 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The calming essential oil in lavender soothes the irritated skin around the affected area, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, the natural antibacterial compounds in the cream inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause infection and prolong healing time. By addressing both inflammation and bacterial growth, English lavender creams provide a gentle and effective solution for relieving stye symptoms and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Herbal English lavender creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacterial infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of sty eyes.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, allowing them to heal more quickly and reducing discomfort.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in English lavender creams may help protect the delicate eye tissue from further damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting healthy recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce redness and swelling associated with stye.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and vitamin E, seeds nourish and soothe the skin around the eye area, promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered lavandula angustifolia with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 24 hours to allow for infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Whip 50 grams of petroleum jelly with the infused oil using an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as geraniol and rosmarinic acid, work together to reduce swelling and redness associated with styes. Additionally, the antimicrobial agents in the cream help combat bacterial infections that can cause styes. By applying a small amount of wild geranium cream directly to the affected area, individuals can experience rapid relief from discomfort, pain, and inflammation, allowing for faster healing and reduced risk of scarring.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Wild geranium creams can help reduce inflammation, redness, and swelling associated with styes by suppressing pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.

Antibacterial and antimicrobial properties: The cream's antibacterial and antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that cause styes, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of recurrence.

Soothing and calming properties: Wild geranium creams' gentle and non-irritating nature can provide instant relief from stye-related discomfort, pain, and itching, while also helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and soothe inflammation associated with sty.
Fruits: Contain tannins that provide antibacterial properties, inhibiting bacterial growth and promoting healing in stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh geranium maculatum leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 3-4 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried geranium maculatum with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking daily to promote extraction.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as artemisinin and thujone, work together to reduce swelling and pain associated with styes. Additionally, wormwood has been shown to have antibacterial effects that help combat the infection causing the stye. By applying the cream topically, it targets the affected area directly, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of the stye. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for alleviating stye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Wormwood's antibacterial compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help to combat bacterial infections that can cause stye, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, including polyacetylenes and coumarins, soothe redness and swelling associated with styes, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: Wormwood's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate the inflammatory response, promoting a faster recovery and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them effective in soothing and treating styes.
Barks: Containing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, they aid in preventing infection and reducing redness and swelling associated with styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves measure 20 grams of dried leaves for every 100ml of cream base.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler heat mixture until melted completely.
Steep the dried artemisia absinthium leaves in hot water for 10 minutes then strain through cheesecloth into the oil mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours or overnight in the refrigerator.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy adding one teaspoon of beeswax if necessary.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with stye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The silymarin in milk thistle has been shown to reduce swelling and redness, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing inflamed eyes caused by a stye. Additionally, the cream's ability to combat bacteria and other microorganisms can help to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing. As a result, milk thistle creams provide fast relief from stye symptoms, promoting comfort and restoring healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with styes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: Neutralizes free radicals that can exacerbate stye symptoms, such as redness, warmth, and pus formation, by protecting skin cells from oxidative damage.

Saponin content: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which helps to combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the development of styes, ultimately promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing swelling associated with sty eyes.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and inflammation caused by styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried silybum marianum flowers and mix with 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 50ml of sweet almond oil and heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and let it infuse for 24 hours allowing the herbal properties to dissolve.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Whip the cream until it cools down slightly then store in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and flavonoids, work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with styes, while also targeting the underlying bacterial infection that causes them. Additionally, dandelion's soothing effects help to calm the skin and promote healing, reducing the risk of further irritation and discomfort. By addressing both the symptoms and cause of a stye, herbal dandelion creams provide effective relief from this common eye condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial compounds in dandelion cream inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause styes, preventing further infection and allowing the body to naturally clear up the condition.

Wound-healing properties: Dandelion's wound-healing properties, including its ability to stimulate collagen production and enhance blood flow, help promote rapid healing of the affected area and reduce the likelihood of scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with stye.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits (achenes) have antimicrobial properties that can combat bacterial infections causing stye.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried taraxacum officinale flowers and one cup of sweet almond oil.
Combine the dried flowers with two cups of boiling water and allow it steep for ten minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted, stirring occasionally, for about twenty-five minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then whip with an electric mixer for several minutes until creamy and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo creams helps with stye because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and isothiocyanates, work together to combat the bacterial infection that causes sty, while also reducing redness, swelling, and pain. Additionally, wild indigo has natural soothing and calming effects on the skin, helping to promote healing and reduce irritation. As a result, applying wild indigo creams to the affected area can provide rapid relief from stye symptoms, promoting faster recovery and alleviating discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and antibacterial properties: Wild indigo cream's antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to reduce bacteria growth, prevent infection, and promote healing in stye-prone areas.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wild indigo cream can help to reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, providing relief from discomfort and promoting faster recovery.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Wild indigo's wound-healing properties can aid in the natural removal of dead skin cells and debris, while its soothing properties can calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with sty eyes.
Barks: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried baptisia tinctoria flowers measuring one part by weight.
Combine harvested flowers with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in ratio of 10:90.
Steep mixture for 4-6 weeks at room temperature shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in proportions of 70:15:15 applying gently.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with stye because they contain potent herbal ingredients that target the root cause of the problem. Eyebright, a natural anti-inflammatory agent, soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with styes. The cream's antimicrobial properties also help to eliminate bacterial infections, preventing the spread of the condition and promoting faster healing. Additionally, eyebright creams often include soothing ingredients like chamomile and calendula, which further ease discomfort and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and redness associated with sty, providing quick relief from discomfort.
Antibacterial property: The antibacterial properties in eyebright cream combat the bacterial infection causing the sty, preventing further inflammation and promoting healing.

Decongestant property: Eyebright cream's decongestant properties help to reduce swelling by reducing fluid retention, allowing for a faster recovery from the sty and alleviating associated discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Eyebright roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with styes.
Seeds: Eyebright seeds contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in soothing stye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers and dry them thoroughly.
Combine 10 grams of dried herb with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes of infusing.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 50 milliliters of coconut oil and heat the mixture over low heat until melted, then combine it with the herb-infused oil and stir well.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, let it cool, and store it in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel-like consistency of licorice cream can be applied directly to the affected area, providing a protective barrier that reduces redness and swelling. The antimicrobial compounds present in licorice also help combat bacterial infections that may cause styes. Additionally, licorice has natural antiviral properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with sty eyes.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial agents present in licorice cream, such as triterpenoids, can help combat the bacterial infection that often causes sty eyes by preventing bacterial growth and promoting healing.

Sothing and soothing agents: Licorice creams often contain soothing ingredients like glycerin, which can help calm irritated skin and reduce discomfort associated with styes, allowing for a more comfortable healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with styes.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to combat infection and reduce symptoms of styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20-30 grams of dried licorice root.
Combine the dried licorice root with 100ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 60 minutes then filter it through cheesecloth.
Mix the filtered liquid with 10g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as hamamelitannin and gallic acid, work together to reduce swelling and redness caused by the infection. Additionally, the cream's astringent properties help to dry out the affected area, promoting the healing process and reducing the risk of further irritation or infection. By applying the cream to the affected eye or eyelid, individuals can experience relief from stye symptoms and promote faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation associated with styes, as well as inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause infection.
Gallotannins: Gallotannins have antiseptic and astringent properties, which help to dry out and heal the infected area, reducing the size and pain of the stye.
Salicylic Acid: Salicylic acid has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling, as well as combat fungal infections that can cause styes.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are rich in tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with styes.
Roots: The roots contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation, making it effective for treating styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers for a calming infusion.
Combine the dried plant material with 4 cups of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids and storing in refrigerator.
Mix one part cooled herbal infusion with three parts cold-pressed coconut oil and whip until smooth for final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow creams helps with stye because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as aloe vera and chamomile, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to gently clear up pus and debris from the affected area, promoting a faster healing process. This natural remedy can provide relief from discomfort and promote a speedy recovery without harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mallow cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes.
Soothing and demulcent properties: The mucilages in mallow cream create a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming irritated areas around the eye and reducing discomfort caused by styes.

Antimicrobial properties: Some herbal mallows, such as Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis), contain compounds that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi, which can help prevent infection and promote healing in cases of sty.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the mallow plant contain mucilage, a natural thickening agent that helps soothe and protect the affected area.
Barks: Mallow bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh malva sylvestris flowers and leaves, drying them thoroughly in a warm place for at least 2 hours.
Combine 20g of dried malva sylvestris with 100ml of distilled water in a heatproof bowl, allowing it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup, squeezing out excess liquid.
Add 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to the filtered liquid, gently heating the mixture over low heat until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer until creamy and smooth, filling sterilized tubes and storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with stye because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness associated with this common eye infection. The cream's antibacterial compounds also combat the bacterial infection that causes the stye, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications like scarring or recurring infections. Additionally, the soothing effects of stinging nettle calm irritated skin, providing relief from discomfort and itching.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle creams help reduce inflammation associated with styes, allowing them to heal faster and minimizing discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in stinging nettle creams protect the affected area from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
Soothing and calming properties: The cream's soothing and calming effects can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with styes, making them more manageable.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin associated with styes.
Seeds: The seeds have antimicrobial properties that aid in preventing infection and promoting healing in affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems, then clean and dry them thoroughly for later use.
Combine the dried plant material with 200ml of boiling water in a saucepan and steep for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the herbal infusion in a double boiler, heating until the wax melts completely.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring the cream into sterilized jars and storing them in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scottish lovage creams helps with stye because its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and mucilages, work together to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that can cause stye. Additionally, lovage creams often contain soothing emollients and humectants that help to moisturize and protect the delicate skin around the eye, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lovage's essential oils have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause sty infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lovage extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate the discomfort associated with sty.
Antiseptic properties: Lovage has been traditionally used to treat skin infections, including sty, due to its antiseptic properties that can help protect the skin from further bacterial invasion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are good for making medicinal creams for stye because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling.
Barks: Barks are used to make medicinal creams for stye because they contain compounds that have antibacterial and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ligusticum scoticum roots and wash them thoroughly with clean water for 30 minutes.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Melt 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter for 20-25 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground ligusticum scoticum powder into the melted oil mixture and stir well to combine.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for stye are natural topical treatments made from plant extracts, such as tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera, which help soothe and calm inflamed eyelids. These creams reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with styes, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of recurrence. Examples of effective herbal creams include those containing calendula, lavender, and green tea extract, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that aid in the treatment of styes. By using these creams, individuals can alleviate symptoms, restore eye comfort, and improve their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stye with herbal creams is to apply a warm compress before applying the cream to reduce swelling. Aloe vera gel, tea tree oil, and chamomile are effective herbs that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and heal the affected area quickly. Apply a thin layer of the herb-infused cream or ointment directly to the stye, three to four times a day, until symptoms resolve.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stye that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, rosacea, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. These natural remedies can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with these skin issues, promoting healthy and clear skin.
